speaker: interviewee
text: Michel Kikoine. K-I-K-O-I-N-E. Born in Gomel, Russia, 1891. Studied painting in the Free Academy of Vilnius with [? Jaime Zutein, ?] and went to Paris in 1912. On the left is 'young girl,' and on the right is 'nude.' A letter from Kikoine to Schneid, written August 16, 1956, and sent from Paris. Page two. The artist Georges Kars, K-A-R-S-- also known as Karpeles, K-A-R-P-E-L-E-S-- was born in Kralupy, near Prague. He studied with Franz von Stuck and went to Paris and Spain and then lived in Paris again. He escaped to Switzerland during World War II. When he learned that, of his many Czechoslovakian relatives, no one remained alive, he became depressed and committed suicide February 5, 1945. On the top left is the painting from 1924. On the top right is a painting called [FRENCH]. On the bottom right is a painting called from 1934. On the middle bottom is a painting from 1922. On the bottom left is a landscape from 1928. A letter from Kars to Schneid, 1930. [BACKGROUND NOISE] A letter to Schneid from 1931. In the bottom, right-hand corner of this letter is a clipping from the Comoedia in 1931, wishing for Kars' speedy recovery from a serious operation. Close up. Page two. A. Iris, I-R-I-S, born in Russia, 1903. In 1924, he went to Paris. These are two paintings from 1930. A letter from 1921. The date of this letter is 1931. Elie Nadelman, N-A-D-E-L-M-A-N. A sculptor. Two pieces from 1917. Alice Hohermann, H-O-H-E-R-M-A-N-N, lived in Berlin and Paris. Shown are three paintings. A letter to Dr. Schneid. A letter to Dr. Schneid from 1931. Page two. Page three. Two invitations from 1929. Max Band, B-A-N-D. Born in Lithuania August 21, 1900. He went to Paris soon after World War I and went to the US in 1940. The painting on the left is called and is from 1928. In the middle is from 1935. On the right is painted between 1949 and '53. painted in 1955. A letter from Band to Schneid, November 1, 1956, sent from California. Page two. K. Harald Isenstein, H-A-R-A-L-D I-S-E-N-S-T-E-I-N. Top left, self-portrait from 1928. Top right, terra cotta, 1925. Bottom left, 1928. Bottom right, bust from 1925. Letter from Isenstein to Schneid, 1930. Letter from Isenstein to Schneid, November 22, 1930. Page two. Elias Newman, . N-E-W-M-A-N, born in the government of Radom, Russian Poland. He went to the US in 1913 and studied in New York. On the left is In the center is a photograph of the artist. On the right is a painting called Two exhibition catalogs. On the left is from 1928, the Baltimore Museum of Art. On the right, from 1930, exhibited in Tel Aviv at the National Citizens Club. A letter written in 1930 and sent from Tel Aviv. Page two. Letter from 1931. Anna Neagoe, N-E-A-G-O-E, nee Frankel, born in 1886 to religious parents in Europe. At age 15, she went to the US. In 1911, she married Peter Nico, a painter and modern writer. In 1926, they went to France. The painting scene is nude. This landscape is a painting of a village in Provence. Letter to Dr. Schneid, sent from France in 1931. Page two. Page three. A review in the October 21, 1929. 'Who's Who Abroad, Anna Neagoe.' Rynecki, R-Y-N-E-C-K-I, born in Poland in 1885. He painted scenes from everyday Jewish life. A letter from Rynecki to Schneid written December 28, 1930 in Yiddish. A letter written in Polish from Rynecki to Schneid, no date. Page two. Leon Droucker, . D-R-O-U-C-K-E-R, born in Vilnius, Lithuania in 1887. Went to France when he was 11 years old. The two sculptures are both from 1931. Letter from Droucker written in French, sent from Paris, October 15, 1931. Page two. Jacob Plessner, P-L-E-S-S-N-E-R, born in Germany, August 18, 1871. On the left is a headstone from 1929 for Gustav [? Bradt. ?] Top left is a sculpture from 1902. Middle top, 1909. Top right is from 1926. Bottom right, 1928, Bottom left, 1928, medallion of Aaron [? Hirsch. ?] A 1930 letter from Berlin from Plessner to Otto Schneid. Nathan Szpigel, S-Z-P-I-G-E-L, born in 1892. On the far left is a painting from 1933. In the middle top is a relief that says [NON-ENGLISH]. To the right of it is a photograph of the artist. And on the far right is a painting from 1935. Bottom right is a sculpture with no date. Bottom left is a sculpture relief entitled [NON-ENGLISH], 1930. A catalog of Nathan Szpigel's work. A list of pieces appearing in the aforementioned catalogue. Letter from Szpigel to Schneid written in 1931. 1936 letter from Szpigel to Schneid. Page two. Bension Rabinovitch, R-A-B-I-N-O-V-I-T-C-H, born in Bialystok, Poland in 1905, exhibited in Vilnius and Warsaw. The painting shown is called A letter written in Yiddish and sent from Paris in 1930. Razanietsky, R-A-Z-A-N-I-E-T-S-K-Y. Eight paintings and drawings from the 1920s and '30s. A letter from Razanietsky to Schneid written from Paris in the Yiddish language in 1930. Pages two and three. [AUDIO OUT]
